title: diasperus carnosulus
side_effects: Diasperus carnosulus can have side effects if used improperly, such as increased heart rate, nausea, and dizziness due to its high concentration of toxic compounds. Prolonged use can lead to liver damage, kidney failure, and even seizures in extreme cases. Additionally, some individuals may experience allergic reactions, such as itching, redness, and swelling, after coming into contact with the plant's sap. If not handled correctly, the plant's volatile oils can cause respiratory problems, including shortness of breath and wheezing.
